yellow_leaves.diagnosis: Check soil moisture first. If waterlogged, improve drainage. Apply urea if nitrogen is deficient. Use ferrous sulfate spray for iron chlorosis.

wilting.diagnosis: Check soil moisture. If dry, irrigate. If wet with discolored roots, it is root rot. Send sample to lab for fungal wilt confirmation.

causes: Fungal infection such as alternaria or helminthosporium
leaf_spots.diagnosis: Observe spot pattern and color. Brown circular spots usually fungal. Angular water-soaked spots usually bacterial. Apply appropriate fungicide.

causes: Nutrient deficiency especially nitrogen and zinc
stunted_growth.diagnosis: Uproot a few plants and inspect roots. Apply zinc sulfate and nitrogen. Test soil compaction by inserting a metal rod.

causes: Dense plant canopy with poor air circulation
white_powdery_coating.diagnosis: This is powdery mildew. Spray sulfur-based fungicide or systemic fungicide. Improve plant spacing.

boll_dropping.diagnosis: Open dropped bolls and inspect for larvae. Apply appropriate insecticide. Check irrigation schedule.

grain_shriveling.diagnosis: Water stress during grain fill is most common. Ensure irrigation at grain filling stage. Check for disease.

lodging.diagnosis: Reduce nitrogen rate. Avoid excess irrigation. Use recommended plant population. Apply potassium which strengthens stems.

causes: High soil pH reducing nutrient uptake
root_rot.diagnosis: Improve field drainage. Avoid excess irrigation. Apply soil fungicide as drench. Test soil pH.

late_blight_potato.diagnosis: Spray copper-based or systemic fungicide immediately. Destroy infected plant material. Use resistant varieties. Do not delay.
